findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 bypasses BLOCKED_HOSTS metadata-IP block
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped IPv6 handling re-checks only is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast on the mapped address, but does NOT re-check it against BLOCKED_HOSTS. BLOCKED_HOSTS contains 100.100.100.200 (Alibaba cloud metadata), a public IP that is none of link-local/loopback/unspecified/multicast. The literal-string check compares the raw hostname '::ffff:100.100.100.200', which is not in the set, so the mapped form of a blocklisted metadata IP passes validation. Verified: '::ffff:100.100.100.200'.ipv4_mapped == 100.100.100.200 and all four is_* flags are False.
findings.fix_hint: also block when str(mapped) in BLOCKED_HOSTS (and mirror the same BLOCKED_HOSTS check on the outer addr) so IPv4-mapped forms of blocklisted metadata IPs are rejected
findings.why_it_matters: An attacker configuring an *arr URL as http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]/ reaches the Alibaba cloud metadata endpoint that BLOCKED_HOSTS is specifically meant to defend against.
source_window: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
# Not an IP literal (e.g. "radarr") -- perfectly fine.
findings.canonical_line_content: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: SSRF bypass via deprecated IPv4-compatible IPv6 notation (::a.b.c.d) not covered by the new ipv4_mapped check
findings.problem: The new mitigation only handles IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:a.b.c.d) via addr.ipv4_mapped. Python's ipaddress module returns None for ipv4_mapped on the older but still-parseable 'IPv4-compatible' IPv6 notation (::a.b.c.d, e.g. ::169.254.169.254 or ::127.0.0.1). Those addresses are also not is_link_local, not is_loopback, not is_unspecified, and not is_multicast on the IPv6Address itself, so they pass both the pre-existing checks and the new one untouched. Verified: validate_arr_url('http://[::169.254.169.254]/') returns (True, ''), and validate_arr_url('http://[::127.0.0.1]/') also returns (True, '').
findings.fix_hint: Also extract and check the embedded IPv4 from the deprecated ::a.b.c.d form (e.g. derive ipaddress.IPv4Address(addr.packed[-4:]) when addr is in ::/96) and apply the same checks; or use addr.is_global / addr.is_private / addr.is_reserved as a broader fallback covering the whole class of IPv4-in-IPv6 encodings.
findings.why_it_matters: An attacker configuring the *arr URL can supply http://[::169.254.169.254]/ or http://[::127.0.0.1]/ to reach cloud-provider instance metadata or loopback services, exfiltrating IAM credentials or hitting internal-only endpoints.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 path skips BLOCKED_HOSTS re-check: Alibaba metadata IP (100.100.100.200) bypasses SSRF filter
findings.problem: The diff closes the IPv4-mapped IPv6 SSRF vector only for link-local/loopback/unspecified/multicast, but does NOT re-check the mapped IPv4 against BLOCKED_HOSTS (the cloud-metadata deny set). Verified at runtime: validate_arr_url('http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:7878') returns (True, '') while the plain form correctly returns (False, 'Blocked hostname').
findings.current_code: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
findings.fix_hint: In the ipv4_mapped branch, also reject when str(mapped) in BLOCKED_HOSTS (re-run the same deny checks applied to the plain hostname, not just the is_* address-class flags).
findings.why_it_matters: This function is the sole SSRF gate before an *arr URL is persisted to config and later used as an HTTP target. An operator- or attacker-supplied URL of the form http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:7878 slips past the exact filter this change hardened.
source_window: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
# Not an IP literal (e.g. "radarr") -- perfectly fine.
findings.canonical_line_content: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: IPv4-mapped addresses bypass explicit blocked metadata IPs
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped branch only checks address classes on the mapped IPv4 value. It never compares the mapped value against BLOCKED_HOSTS, so a blocked endpoint like 100.100.100.200 is rejected as raw IPv4 but accepted as http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:7878. That defeats the explicit metadata-service blocklist for any environment where IPv4-mapped IPv6 connects to the IPv4 target.
findings.fix_hint: After computing mapped, compare str(mapped) against BLOCKED_HOSTS before allowing the URL, and add a regression test for http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:7878.
findings.why_it_matters: Defeats the explicit metadata-service blocklist via IPv4-mapped IPv6 encoding.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
